summary: 2026-07-01 11:58:04 PDT - Not sound as-is. The user-facing 7.1.0+ warning is present, but the branch still falsely closes the esbuild/Vite item on an invalid dependency graph and leaves active roadmap guidance that contradicts the dropped live-test/surface-gate decision. I could not verify `npm test` or `npm run build` in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `.vite-temp` files and hit EPERM.

findings.title: Dependency closure is not backed by a clean graph
findings.claim: The new DONE closure says esbuild resolves to a single 0.25.12 across the tree and that the lockfile/gate are clean, with the same overclaim repeated in `dev-docs/roadmap.md:485`. But `npm ls vite esbuild --all` exits `ELSPROBLEMS`: `vitest@4.1.8` pulls `vite@8.0.16`, which requires esbuild `^0.27.0 || ^0.28.0`, while the installed tree dedupes esbuild `0.25.12` and marks it invalid. That violates AC6's verified clean-state requirement and closes a dependency/security backlog item on a broken graph.
findings.suggestion: Either reconcile the manifest/lock so `npm ls vite esbuild --all` is clean, or narrow the DONE prose to the actually verified advisory state and track the Vitest/Vite/esbuild peer conflict as a separate open item.

findings.title: Dropped gate still exists as roadmap guidance
findings.claim: The branch adds a top-level decision saying the live-test matrix is DROPPED, but the Current state table still says rendering should be re-confirmed via the live-test matrix. The same roadmap also keeps the Phase 6A camera-surface gate as unconfirmed and tied to that matrix at lines 344-348, while the newly added matrix/guide state that the surface-gate question is resolved. This leaves competing sources of truth and can resurrect the gate AC5 says was dropped.
findings.suggestion: Update `dev-docs/roadmap.md` consistently: change the Current state row to accepted risk for clients below 7.1.0 with no matrix, and either remove the Phase 6A camera-surface item as resolved or restate it as a plain Marketplace configuration check with no live-test-matrix dependency.
